explainability.findings: Policy documents describe the right for individuals to learn about algorithmic logic used in automated decision-making and profiling.
explainability.path_to_improvement: Publish user-facing explanation interfaces or documented appeal workflows.

relevant_sources.summary: The Dick's Sporting Goods Park Privacy Policy provides evidence for **explainability, governance, oversight, privacy, and transparency**. This policy document supports these pillars by describing the venue's use of automated decision-making and profiling, including the right for individuals to opt-out and learn about algorithmic logic. It also details data handling for technologies like facial recognition and session replay, and outlines practices for targeted advertising and user profile customization, all of which necessitate transparency and governance.
relevant_sources.title: Dick's Sporting Goods Park Privacy Policy

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.findings: Technical papers highlight the risks of biased customer reviews impacting employment decisions. These papers also address legal responsibility for discriminatory outcomes in automated systems.
fairness.path_to_improvement: Publish bias testing results, outcome monitoring, or vendor fairness certifications.

relevant_sources.summary: This technical paper discusses algorithmic discrimination and antidiscrimination protections, providing evidence for the **fairness** pillar by highlighting the risks of biased customer reviews impacting employment decisions. It also supports the **governance** pillar by addressing legal responsibility for discriminatory outcomes in automated systems and mentioning the use of algorithms for employment decisions.
relevant_sources.title: Discrimination by Algorithm: Employer Accountability for Biased Customer Reviews

governance.findings: Technical papers address legal responsibility for discriminatory outcomes in automated systems and reference the use of algorithms for employment decisions. Reports reference the use of an AI talent intelligence platform for creating a complete picture of talent by identifying skills, aligning data, and managing data sources for AI-driven profiling. Additionally, policy documents describe the use of automated decision-making and profiling, detailing data handling for technologies like facial recognition and session replay, and outlining practices for targeted advertising and user profile customization.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: This technical paper discusses algorithmic discrimination and antidiscrimination protections, providing evidence for the **fairness** pillar by highlighting the risks of biased customer reviews impacting employment decisions. relevant_sources.title: Discrimination by Algorithm: Employer Accountability for Biased Customer Reviews

relevant_sources.summary: This third-party report on Dick's Sporting Goods' use of the Eightfold AI talent intelligence platform provides evidence for the **governance** pillar. The report implies governance through the platform's creation of a "complete picture of talent" by identifying and inferring skills from employee and applicant profiles, aligning data to skills and proficiency levels, and managing data sources for AI-driven profiling.

summary.overall_findings: Dick's Sporting Goods' policy documents describe the venue's use of automated decision-making and profiling, alongside the right for individuals to opt-out of such processes. These disclosures, which address 6 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars, also detail data handling for technologies such as facial recognition and session replay under the privacy pillar. Further materials describe the right for individuals to learn about algorithmic logic within explainability, and technical papers highlight the risks of biased customer reviews impacting employment decisions for fairness. No qualifying public evidence was found for external accountability. This assessment draws on 11 publicly available sources.
